abstract: A list of rules, together with a context for evaluating them and interpreting results, for use in constructing data-driven logic or fuzzy logic systems.
overview:
## Overview

A [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem] object manages a list of rules ([doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule] objects). A rule system also offers methods for evaluating its list of rules in a context defined by two features: a [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem/state] dictionary containing information to be tested by rules, and a set of [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem/facts] representing the conclusions drawn as a result of rule evaluation. You can evaluate facts based on a binary truth state—that is, a fact either is or is not in the set—or on a continuously variable membership grade, representing different levels of veracity, confidence, or strength for use in fuzzy logic.

You construct a rule system by creating [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule] objects and adding them to the system’s list of rules. There are multiple ways to construct rules: for greater reusability, use the methods listed in Creating Data-Driven Rules; or for greater flexibility, use the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/init(blockPredicate:action:)] method or create a custom subclass of [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule] or [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKNSPredicateRule]. Then, add rules to the system with the methods listed in Managing a System’s List of Rules below.

To evaluate a system, call the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem/evaluate()] method. This method processes each rule in the system in the order it appears in the system’s [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem/agenda] list. You set this order with the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/salience] property of each rule, or with the order in which you add rules to the system. As the system processes each rule, it tests the rule’s [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/evaluatePredicate(in:)] method to determine whether the rule is satisfied in the context of the system. If the rule’s predicate is satisfied, the system executes the rule’s [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/performAction(in:)] method and moves the rule to the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem/executed] list (so the further evaluation of the agenda doesn’t repeatedly trigger the rule’s action).

Rules typically use the system’s [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem/state] dictionary as input and its set of [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem/facts] as output. (However, more complex systems can include sets of rules whose predicates test facts or whose actions mutate the system’s state.) After evaluating a rule system, you can examine the set of facts it has produced using the methods listed in Drawing Conclusions from Facts below. You can then use the presence of a fact in the set, the value of its membership grade, or the combined membership grades of a group of facts to influence the behaviors in your game.

For more information about rules and rule systems, read [https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/General/Conceptual/GameplayKit_Guide/RuleSystems.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP40015172-CH10] in [https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/General/Conceptual/GameplayKit_Guide/index.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP40015172].

# GKRuleSystem

**Class**

A list of rules, together with a context for evaluating them and interpreting results, for use in constructing data-driven logic or fuzzy logic systems.

## Declaration

```swift
class GKRuleSystem
```

## Overview

A [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem] object manages a list of rules ([doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule] objects). A rule system also offers methods for evaluating its list of rules in a context defined by two features: a [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem/state] dictionary containing information to be tested by rules, and a set of [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem/facts] representing the conclusions drawn as a result of rule evaluation. You can evaluate facts based on a binary truth state—that is, a fact either is or is not in the set—or on a continuously variable membership grade, representing different levels of veracity, confidence, or strength for use in fuzzy logic.

You construct a rule system by creating [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule] objects and adding them to the system’s list of rules. There are multiple ways to construct rules: for greater reusability, use the methods listed in Creating Data-Driven Rules; or for greater flexibility, use the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/init(blockPredicate:action:)] method or create a custom subclass of [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule] or [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKNSPredicateRule]. Then, add rules to the system with the methods listed in Managing a System’s List of Rules below.

To evaluate a system, call the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem/evaluate()] method. This method processes each rule in the system in the order it appears in the system’s [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem/agenda] list. You set this order with the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/salience] property of each rule, or with the order in which you add rules to the system. As the system processes each rule, it tests the rule’s [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/evaluatePredicate(in:)] method to determine whether the rule is satisfied in the context of the system. If the rule’s predicate is satisfied, the system executes the rule’s [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/performAction(in:)] method and moves the rule to the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem/executed] list (so the further evaluation of the agenda doesn’t repeatedly trigger the rule’s action).

Rules typically use the system’s [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem/state] dictionary as input and its set of [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem/facts] as output. (However, more complex systems can include sets of rules whose predicates test facts or whose actions mutate the system’s state.) After evaluating a rule system, you can examine the set of facts it has produced using the methods listed in Drawing Conclusions from Facts below. You can then use the presence of a fact in the set, the value of its membership grade, or the combined membership grades of a group of facts to influence the behaviors in your game.

For more information about rules and rule systems, read [https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/General/Conceptual/GameplayKit_Guide/RuleSystems.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP40015172-CH10] in [https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/General/Conceptual/GameplayKit_Guide/index.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP40015172].

## Creating a Rule System

- **init()**: Initializes a new, empty rule system.

## Managing State Information

- **state**: A dictionary of state information to be evaluated by the system’s rules.

## Managing a System’s List of Rules

- **rules**: The list of rules to be executed when evaluating the system.
- **add(_:)**: Adds the specified rule to the system.
- **add(_:)**: Adds the specified list of rules to the system.
- **removeAllRules()**: Removes all rules from the system.

## Evaluating a Rule System

- **evaluate()**: Evaluates the rule system, executing the list of rules in its agenda.
- **agenda**: The list of rules to be considered when evaluating the system.
- **executed**: The list of rules whose actions have been performed during evaluation of the system.
- **reset()**: Returns the rule system to its original agenda and clears all facts.

## Asserting and Retracting Facts

- **facts**: The list of facts claimed by the rule system.
- **assertFact(_:)**: Adds the specified fact to the fact set with a membership grade of 1.0, and reevaluates the rules in the system’s agenda.
- **assertFact(_:grade:)**: Increases the membership grade of the specified fact by the specified amount, adding it to the fact set if necessary, and reevaluates the rules in the system’s agenda.
- **retractFact(_:)**: Removes the specified fact from the fact set, and reevaluates the rules in the system’s agenda.
- **retractFact(_:grade:)**: Reduces the membership grade of the specified fact by the specified amount, removing it from the fact set if necessary, and reevaluates the rules in the system’s agenda.

## Drawing Conclusions from Facts

- **grade(forFact:)**: Returns the membership grade of the specified fact.
- **minimumGrade(forFacts:)**: Returns the lowest membership grade among the specified facts.
- **maximumGrade(forFacts:)**: Returns the highest membership grade among the specified facts.

## Rule Systems

- **GKRule**: A rule to be used in the context of a rule system, with a predicate to be tested and an action to be executed when the test succeeds.
- **GKNSPredicateRule**: A rule for use in a rule system that uses a Foundation [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Foundation/NSPredicate] object to evaluate itself.

## Inherits From

- NSObject

## Conforms To

- CVarArg
- CustomDebugStringConvertible
- CustomStringConvertible
- Equatable
- Hashable
- NSObjectProtocol
